title: Jayson Tatum says its ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum called the trade of Jaylen Brown to the 76ers 'weird' and acknowledged the emotional impact on Boston fans.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and four draft picks, ending his partnership with Tatum.
Jaylen Brown expressed mixed emotions about moving to Philadelphia, highlighting the shock of the trade decision.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN writer Bill Barnwell values Patriots' quarterback Drake Maye at six first-round picks, suggesting high trade potential.
Drake Maye, at 23, reached Super Bowl runner-up and earned two Pro Bowl selections, marking significant early career success.
Notable NFL QB trades include Deshaun Watson for three first-round picks and Eli Manning for Phillip Rivers and draft picks.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Furnished island cottage on Lake Winnipesaukee in Gilford, N.H., offers rental potential.
Built in 1952, the 1,037 sq. ft. home has 3 bedrooms, 1 bath, private sewer and water.
The property is listed for $889,500; 2025 taxes are $8,027.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' features Matt Damon as Odysseus, presenting a human-centered retelling of Homer's classic epic.
The movie departs from the original by focusing on Odysseus's humanity over mythological elements, using a non-linear narrative.
'The Odyssey' highlights Odysseus's complex character, blending personal flaws with epic adventures, and is noted for its practical effects.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle married his partner Devin during the All-Star break, with celebrations at Arya Trattoria.
The couple shared their engagement news in March and have been together publicly since at least June 2020.
Tolle, 23, was drafted by the Red Sox in 2024 and has a 5-7 record with a 3.59 ERA in 18 starts.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore lead Patriots' defensive line with 86 combined pressures last season.
Patriots ranked sixth in the league, allowing 101.7 rushing yards per game last season.
Patriots face challenges on edge depth, with Harold Landry's injury and Khyiris Tonga's departure affecting lineup.

title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: 28-year-old Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam died in a skydiving accident in Orange, Massachusetts, due to a gust of wind.
The mishap at Jumptown Skydiving involved an unpredictable weather phenomenon known as a dust devil interfering with Gaddam's parachute.
Gaddam, a Northeastern University graduate, recently earned his skydiving A License and worked as a software developer.

title: Is the real summer turnaround in Boston sports coming from Legacy FC?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC secured its first road win, defeating Orlando Pride 1-0 in NWSL action.
After an 0-5 season start, Legacy FC is on a winning streak, vying for a playoff spot.
Legacy FC will face Washington Spirit, second in the league, at Centreville Bank Stadium on Sunday.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 16, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Nana's Green Tea opens July 17 on Newbury Street, offering a variety of matcha-based drinks and Japanese dishes.
About Thyme Lebanese Street Café in Harvard Square will open August 1, featuring saj flatbreads and labneh.
C’Yool Yemeni Café and Bakery in the South End begins operations in July with homemade coffee and Yemeni pastries.

title: 19-year-old dies in apparent drowning in Merrimack River
author: John Waller
bullets: 19-year-old Robert Batchelder of Manchester, NH, died after drowning in the Merrimack River in Canterbury.
Batchelder was swimming with a co-worker when he struggled and went underwater; Concord firefighters recovered his body.
Authorities do not consider the death suspicious but are investigating the circumstances surrounding the incident.
